lines.gurmukhi: ਚਉਥੇ ਪਦ ਮਹਿ ਜਨ ਕੀ ਜਿੰਦੁ ॥੪॥੧॥੪॥
lines.translit: chauthe pad meh jan kee jind |4|1|4|
lines.english: The soul of His humble servant dwells in the fourth state. ||4||1||4||
Says Kabeer, such is my Lord and Master.
Even then, his hardened mind was not satisfied.
Gond:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਇਹੁ ਮਾਨਸੁ ਨਾ ਇਹੁ ਦੇਉ ॥
lines.translit: naa ihu maanas naa ihu deo |
lines.english: It is not human, and it is not a god.
It is not called celibate, or a worshipper of Shiva.
It is not a Yogi, and it is not a hermit.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਕਾ ਅੰਤੁ ਨ ਕੋਊ ਪਾਈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: taa kaa ant na koaoo paaee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: No one can find its limits. ||1||Pause||
Then what is it, which dwells in this temple of the body?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਇਸੁ ਮਾਇ ਨ ਕਾਹੂ ਪੂਤਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naa is maae na kaahoo pootaa |1|
lines.english: It is not a mother, or anyone's son. ||1||
It is not a house-holder, and it is not a renouncer of the world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਇਹੁ ਰਾਜ ਨ ਭੀਖ ਮੰਗਾਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: naa ihu raaj na bheekh mangaasee |
lines.english: It is not a king, and it is not a beggar.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਇਸੁ ਪਿੰਡੁ ਨ ਰਕਤੂ ਰਾਤੀ ॥
lines.translit: naa is pindd na rakatoo raatee |
lines.english: It has no body, no drop of blood.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਸੁ ਮਰਤੇ ਕਉ ਜੇ ਕੋਊ ਰੋਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: eis marate kau je koaoo rovai |
lines.english: If someone cries over its death,

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਇਹੁ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਨੁ ਨਾ ਇਹੁ ਖਾਤੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naa ihu brahaman naa ihu khaatee |2|
lines.english: It is not a Brahmin, and it is not a Kh'shaatriya. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਇਹੁ ਜੀਵੈ ਨ ਮਰਤਾ ਦੇਖੁ ॥
lines.translit: naa ihu jeevai na marataa dekh |
lines.english: It does not live, and it is not seen to die.
It is not called a man of austere self-discipline, or a Shaykh.
that person loses his honor. ||3||
By Guru's Grace, I have found the Path.
Birth and death have both been erased.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਕਬੀਰ ਇਹੁ ਰਾਮ ਕੀ ਅੰਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: kahu kabeer ihu raam kee ans |
lines.english: Says Kabeer, this is formed of the same essence as the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਸ ਕਾਗਦ ਪਰ ਮਿਟੈ ਨ ਮੰਸੁ ॥੪॥੨॥੫॥
lines.translit: jas kaagad par mittai na mans |4|2|5|
lines.english: It is like the ink on the paper which cannot be erased. ||4||2||5||
The threads are broken, and the starch has run out.
Gond:
Bare reeds glisten at the front door.
The poor brushes are scattered in pieces.
Death has entered this shaven head. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਵਤ ਜਾਤ ਨਾਕ ਸਰ ਹੋਈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: aavat jaat naak sar hoee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: All this coming and going has irritated him. ||1||Pause||
This shaven-headed mendicant has wasted all his wealth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਵਾ ਕਾ ਮਨੁ ਰਾਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: raam naam vaa kaa man raataa |
lines.english: His mind is attuned to the Lord's Name.
His daughters and sons have nothing to eat,
He has given up all talk of his weaving equipment.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਮ ਕਉ ਸਾਥਰੁ ਉਨ ਕਉ ਖਾਟ ॥
lines.translit: ham kau saathar un kau khaatt |
lines.english: We sleep on the floor, while they sleep in the beds.
while the shaven-headed mendicants night and day eat their fill. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕ ਦੁਇ ਮੰਦਰਿ ਇਕ ਦੁਇ ਬਾਟ ॥
lines.translit: eik due mandar ik due baatt |
lines.english: One or two are in the house, and one or two more are on the way.
They rub their bare heads, and carry prayer-books in their waist-bands.
They are the support of the drowning.
He will become one of these shaven-headed mendicants.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਮ ਕਉ ਚਾਬਨੁ ਉਨ ਕਉ ਰੋਟੀ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: ham kau chaaban un kau rottee |3|
lines.english: We get dry grains, while they get loaves of bread. ||3||
Listen, O blind and unguided Loi:
Kabeer has taken shelter with these shaven-headed mendicants. ||4||3||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਸਮੁ ਮਰੈ ਤਉ ਨਾਰਿ ਨ ਰੋਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: khasam marai tau naar na rovai |
lines.english: When her husband dies, the woman does not cry.
When this protector dies,
he falls into the world of hell hereafter, for the sexual pleasures he enjoyed in this world. ||1||
Someone else becomes her protector.
Gond:
The world loves only the one bride, Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਗਲੇ ਜੀਅ ਜੰਤ ਕੀ ਨਾਰੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sagale jeea jant kee naaree |1| rahaau |
lines.english: She is the wife of all beings and creatures. ||1||Pause||
She is poison to the Saint, but the world is delighted with her.
Adorning herself, she sits like a prostitute.
With her necklace around her neck, this bride looks beautiful.
She is afraid of being beaten by those blessed with the Guru's Grace.
She is the body, the breath of life, of the faithless cynics.
Cursed by the Saints, she wanders around like a wretch. ||2||
She runs around, chasing after the Saints.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਮ ਕਉ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਪਰੈ ਤ੍ਰਖਿ ਡਾਇਣਿ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: ham kau drisatt parai trakh ddaaein |3|
lines.english: She appears to me like a blood-thirsty witch. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਮ ਤਿਸ ਕਾ ਬਹੁ ਜਾਨਿਆ ਭੇਉ ॥
lines.translit: ham tis kaa bahu jaaniaa bheo |
lines.english: I know her secrets well
in His Mercy, the Divine Guru met me.
She clings to the skirt of the world. ||4||4||7||
Says Kabeer, now I have thrown her out.
